---
title: "Sofia Sarak"
image: images/channel_headshot.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  #image-width: 
  links:
    - text: LinkedIn
      icon: linkedin
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/sofiasarak
      target: _blank # ensures that link is opened in a new browser tab
    - text: GitHub
      icon: github
      href: https://github.com/sofiasarak
      target: -blank
    - text: Email
      icon: envelope
      href: mailto:sarak@ucsb.edu
---

<h1>Welcome! I'm Sofia. </h1>

I am an early-career environmental data scientist passionate about data communication, visualization, and statistical analysis. I recently completed a Master of Environmental Data Science at the Bren School of Environmental Science & Management (UCSB), and am currently searching for roles in NYC! Click around my wesbite to learn more about me and all of my fun little projects :)

::: panel-tabset
### Education

**Master of Environmental Data Science** (June 2026) <br>Bren School of Environmental Science & Management @ University of California, Santa Barbara (UCSB)

**Bachelor of Arts in Earth & Environmental Sciences, Economics** (2025) <br> Wesleyan University @ Middletown, CT

### Projects

**Water Quality Data Interactive Viewer** (July 2026 - Present) <br> Westport Conservation Department (Westport, CT)

**Power lines and people -- Mapping how distribution grid constraints shape resilient and equitable energy transition** (Completed June 2026) <br> *Master's Capstone Project* <br> Bren School of Environmental Science & Management

**Ants, Plants, and Isotopes: Investigating the Trophic Levels of the Guánica Dry Forest** (Completed March 2025) <br> *Earth & Environmental Sciences Senior Capstone Project*  <br> Wesleyan University 

### Experience

**Career Development Student Assistant** (2025 - Present) <br> Bren School of Environmental Science & Management

**Lab Intern** (2023 - 2025) <br> College of the Environment, Wesleyan University

**Course Assistant** (2023 - 2024) <br> Economics Department, Wesleyan University

**Field and Lab Intern** (2023) <br> Harbor Watch at Earthplace (Westport, CT)

For more information about these roles, [check out my resume](docs/SofiaSarak_Resume.pdf){target="_blank"}!

### Fun Facts!

{{< fa earth-europe title="descriptive text for accessibility" >}}  I'm first-generation American, which has shown me the importance of language for communication and connection. **I grew up speaking Russian and Ukrainian at home**, and am very determined to not lose the Spanish I learned in high school and college.

{{< fa book-bookmark title="descriptive text for accessibility" >}}  **I love to read!** Some recent favorites include [Educated](https://www.goodreads.com/book/show/35133922-educated) by Tara Westover and [Happy City](https://www.goodreads.com/book/show/13330588-happy-city?from_search=true&from_srp=true&qid=K5OBEgo4XG&rank=1) by Charles Montgomery.

{{< fa person-swimming title="descriptive text for accessibility" >}}  When I'm not coding, practicing my Spanish, or reading, I'm probably doing some sort of physical activity. Like many environmental scientists, **I enjoy hiking, but I'm also a big fan of weightlifting and swimming laps at the pool**.
:::
